channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Are Europe’s extreme summers the new normal? What the science says
item.description: WHO warns Europe must 'plan for heat like winter flu' as experts reveal how permanent this summer's extreme heat is.

item.title: Delcy Rodriguez responds to public anger at government response
item.description: Delcy Rodriguez says 80% of the buildings that collapsed in earthquakes were privately developed.

item.title: Switzerland beat Algeria 2-0 to reach World Cup Round of 16 in style
item.description: Switzerland remain unbeaten at the 2026 World Cup and will face either Colombia or Ghana in the next game.

item.title: Khamenei’s coffin readied as Iran prepares for seven-day funeral
item.description: Former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's coffin has been readied as Iran prepares for seven-day funeral.

item.title: Mapping Iran’s Ali Khamenei funeral: Where mourners will gather each day
item.description: Funeral processions for Iran's late supreme leader will take place between July 3-9 across Iran and Iraq.

item.title: Canada’s Carney secures deal for pipeline to expand oil exports beyond US
item.description: Ottawa says future pipeline will reduce economic dependence on the US amid Donald Trump's trade war.

item.title: Papua rebels claim to have killed US pilot in Indonesia
item.description: A US pilot has been killed in Indonesia’s Papua region after separatist rebels said they shot him.

item.title: Iran warns ships against using unapproved routes in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Military command issues threat a day after Qatari mediators hailed 'positive progress' in indirect US-Iranian talks.

item.title: Who are the rogue bishops defying Pope Leo XIV?
item.description: A rebel group of Roman Catholics has ordained its own bishops in direct defiance of Pope Leo XIV and the Vatican order.

item.title: ‘They gave their best’: Congolese reflect on historic World Cup run
item.description: The Leopards' first World Cup in more than 50 years united Congolese supporters, despite defeat to England.

item.title: Powerful explosion as firefighters probe smoke at Tacoma apartment
item.description: Powerful explosion as firefighters probe smoke at Tacoma apartment.

item.title: Ronaldo scores as Portugal come back to win, Croatia denied by late VAR
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo pulls Portugal level before Goncalo Ramos wins it late on; Croatia have further goal ruled offside.

item.title: Relief efforts shift as Venezuela quake survivors seek aid
item.description: Emergency relief is becoming the focus in Venezuela’s La Guaira state as aid stations distribute food.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran slams US before huge funeral for Ali Khamenei
item.description: Lebanon's Health Ministry says at least 4,298 people have been killed and 12,196 injured in Israeli attacks since March.

item.title: Trump administration indicts Olympic athlete for Reflecting Pool vandalism
item.description: David Hearn could face up to 10 years in prison if convicted, but critics question the legitimacy of Trump's claims.

item.title: Will the end of TPS for Haitians mean a caregiving crisis in US?
item.description: Immigrants with temporary protection more likely to work in healthcare, representing 15 percent of noncitizen workers.

item.title: Portugal fans gather ahead of Ronaldo-Modric clash
item.description: Portugal fans gather ahead of Ronaldo-Modric clash.

item.title: Senegal’s World Cup agony: Nation left rueing last-gasp collapse
item.description: Former players and fans blame substitutions, lack of focus, and travel restrictions for Senegal's World Cup exit.

item.title: Oyarzabal scores two goals as Spain dominates Austria in World Cup knockout
item.description: Mikel Oyarzabal's brace allows the European champions to dominate Austria and move on to the round of 16.

item.title: Could water become a flashpoint between Islamabad and New Delhi?
item.description: Pakistan has warned India over the Indus Water Treaty.

item.title: WHO declares cruise-linked hantavirus outbreak officially over
item.description: WHO declares cruise-linked hantavirus outbreak over as final exposed person completes quarantine and tests negative.

item.title: UK’s likely next leader Andy Burnham vows to fully fund defence plans
item.description: Andy Burnham says the UK must take the defence investment plan 'very seriously' despite 4.7bn-pound funding hole.

item.title: Trump administration aims to cut regulations on US commercial fishing
item.description: Scallop fishing had been banned in the New England waters since 1994 on account of overfishing.

item.title: UK culture minister quits X over ‘abuse and misinformation’
item.description: UK Department of Culture, Media and Sport will also stop using social media platform.

item.title: Violent clashes as Albania’s anti-government protests continue
item.description: Violent clashes as Albania’s anti-government protests continue
